question: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in each year from 2016 through 2019 and also had 2020 Census populations under 100,000? List the city names in alphabetical order separated by `, `. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities 2016 release, 10 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, and San Antonio.
1.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities 2016 release?

2.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities 2017 release, 12 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Baytown, Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Houston, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, and San Antonio.
2.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities 2017 release?

3.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities 2018 release, 12 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Houston, Laredo, Longview, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio, and Tyler.
3.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities 2018 release?

4.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities 2019 release, 13 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Laredo, Longview, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio, Tyler, and Waco.
4.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities 2019 release?

5.fact: According to the 2020 Census Redistricting Data for All Texas Places, the following 2020 Census populations (POP100 field) are recorded for the 9 cities from the intersection: Beaumont (115,282), Brownsville (186,738), Corpus Christi (317,863), El Paso (678,815), Laredo (255,205), McAllen (142,210), Mission (85,778), Pharr (79,715), and San Antonio (1,434,625). Only Mission and Pharr have populations under 100,000.
5.subquestion: Which cities from <intersection of nodes 1-4: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio> had population under 100,000 in the 2020 Census?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2016 release
Node 2: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2017 release
Node 3: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2018 release
Node 4: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2019 release
HOP 2 (d=1, data.gov filter - node 5 depends on hop 1):
Node 5: 2020 Census populations for the 9 cities -> under 100,000: Mission, Pharr
